source: Because the scope grew after the last alignment call, I separate urgent tasks from noisy ones and assign fixed windows to each stream based on the clarified ownership, without letting uncertainty leak into the plan.
Because the first draft exposed several gaps, we lock the interface contract before touching downstream behavior ahead of the next review, without burning extra time on avoidable rework.
After we mapped the dependencies in detail, I bundle related updates so stakeholders can react without context switching ahead of the next review, so reviewers can react quickly when it matters.

Once the validation environment is finally stable, I separate urgent tasks from noisy ones and assign fixed windows to each stream without adding another escalation path, while deadlines continue to tighten.

After the third round of revisions stabilized the spec, we verify every external dependency before promising a delivery date against the revised milestone, without letting uncertainty leak into the plan.

Once the validation environment is finally stable, I separate urgent tasks from noisy ones and assign fixed windows to each stream at a manageable coordination cost, while deadlines continue to tighten.
